1.q: HtNHX1 and HtNHX2 have identical 269 bp of 5′ UTR and 501 bp of 3′UTR... their coding sequences are also identical except a segment of 342 bp in HtNHX1 encoding 114 amino acids which is absent in HtNHX2.

2.q: we report the cloning of a Salt Overly Sensitive 1 (SOS1) gene encoding a plasma membrane Na+/H+ antiporter from a highly salt-tolerant genotype of H. tuberosus, named HtSOS1

3.q: both HtNHX1 and HtNHX2 are classified as IC type‐I NHXs... Type‐I NHXs are vacuolar‐located, driving the excess cytosolic Na+ into the vacuole.

4.q: both HtNHX1 and HtNHX2 are classified as IC type‐I NHXs... Type‐I NHXs are vacuolar‐located, driving the excess cytosolic Na+ into the vacuole.

5.q: the transgenic rice expressed with HtSOS1 could exclude more Na+ and accumulate more K+.

6.q: the overexpression of HtNHX2 resulted in significant increases in shoot biomass (70%), root biomass (35%) and plant K+ contents (25%–55%).

7.q: we report the cloning of a Salt Overly Sensitive 1 (SOS1) gene encoding a plasma membrane Na+/H+ antiporter from a highly salt-tolerant genotype of H. tuberosus, named HtSOS1
